stories.headline: Ongoing Sahel conflict displaces additional 150,000 in past month
stories.summary: UN refugee agency figures show continued displacement across the central Sahel as armed-group activity expands. The story receives minimal coverage in major US outlets.

perspectives.keyFraming: African outlets cover the regional impact and the failure of regional security arrangements.
perspectives.summary: Regional outlets focus on the breakdown of the Liptako-Gourma security framework and the impact on rural agricultural communities. Coverage emphasizes humanitarian access constraints and the limited international attention to the crisis.

sources.title: Sahel displacement crisis deepens as security framework falters

perspectives.keyFraming: European outlets cover the story primarily through the lens of migration risk to Europe.
perspectives.summary: Coverage in European outlets notes the displacement figures and frames them around concerns about onward migration toward North Africa and Europe. Humanitarian framing varies by outlet.

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire coverage exists but receives limited US placement.
perspectives.summary: AP and Reuters carried the UN figures with brief dispatches. None of the major US cable or network broadcasts led with the story; coverage was relegated to back-of-segment or wire-service mentions.

sources.title: UN: 150,000 displaced in Sahel over past month
